grace:verification-plan.verification_suite:
config/global.yaml §tokenizer.quality_gates
<verification id="V-M-TOKENIZE-03" description="Reconstruction MSE: fidelity of OHLCV round-trip through VQ-VAE" command="python -m src.core.kronos.tokenizer --input data/v3/processed --output data/v3/tokens --quality-check" expected="recon_mse <= 0.01 (normalized OHLCV space)" stop_on_fail="false" module="M-TOKENIZE" note="If > 0.05: reduce codebook bits, may indicate domain shift. If 0.01-0.05: proceed with caution, monitor Phase 2 metrics." />
<verification id="V-M-TOKENIZE-04" description="Per-asset minimum unique codes: ensure no ticker collapses to < 50 codes" command="python -m src.core.kronos.tokenizer --input data/v3/processed --output data/v3/tokens --quality-check" expected="all 21 assets >= 50 unique codes each" stop_on_fail="true" module="M-TOKENIZE" note="If any asset < 20: remove from universe. If 20-50: flag, may be low-volatility asset." />
<verification id="V-M-TOKENIZE-05" description="Cross-sectional RMS correlation: assets must be differentiable" command="python -m src.core.kronos.tokenizer --input data/v3/processed --output data/v3/tokens --quality-check" expected="RMS correlation < 0.5 across 21-asset return matrix" stop_on_fail="false" module="M-TOKENIZE" note="If > 0.7: apply sector-neutral z-scoring. All assets moving together → no cross-sectional spread." />

config/global.yaml §training
<verification id="V-M-FINETUNE-01" description="CE loss convergence: validation CE monotonically decreases over 30 epochs" command="python src/core/kronos/fine_tune.py --resume kronos_moex_best.pt; grep val_ce checkpoints/kronos_moex/history.json" expected="Val CE at epoch 30 < Val CE at epoch 5 (decreasing trend)" stop_on_fail="true" module="M-FINE-TUNE" />
<verification id="V-M-FINETUNE-02" description="Train CE vs Val CE gap: check for overfitting" command="python -c \"import json; d=json.load(open('checkpoints/kronos_moex/history.json')); gap=abs(d['train_ce'][-1]-d['val_ce'][-1]); print(gap)\"" expected="Train CE - Val CE gap < 0.5 (not overfitting). If gap > 0.5: add dropout 0.2" stop_on_fail="true" module="M-FINE-TUNE" />
<verification id="V-M-FINETUNE-03" description="Val CE vs random baseline: model must beat random token prediction" command="python -c \"import json; d=json.load(open('checkpoints/kronos_moex/history.json')); ce=d['val_ce'][-1]; random_ce=10.0; assert ce < 0.9 * random_ce, f'CE {ce} >= {0.9*random_ce}'\"" expected="Val CE < 0.9 * random_CE (random_CE ~= 10.0 for 1024-class)" stop_on_fail="true" module="M-FINE-TUNE" note="If val CE >= random CE: model not learning — check tokenizer, data quality." />
<verification id="V-M-FINETUNE-05" description="Gradient norm in reasonable range" command="tail -100 logs/fine_tune.log | grep 'grad_norm'" expected="grad_norm > 0.01 and < 50.0 (not vanishing, not exploding)" stop_on_fail="false" module="M-FINE-TUNE" />
<verification id="V-M-FINETUNE-06" description="Checkpoint resume integrity: resume produces same loss trajectory" command="diff <(python fine_tune.py --epochs 5) <(python fine_tune.py --epochs 3; python fine_tune.py --resume --epochs 2) | grep val_ce" expected="Resumed run matches fresh run val_CE within 1e-6 tolerance" stop_on_fail="false" module="M-FINE-TUNE" />
<verification id="V-M-FINETUNE-07" description="Per-asset CE tracking: no single asset dominates loss improvement" command="grep 'per_asset_ce' checkpoints/kronos_moex/history.json" expected="Std dev of per-asset val CE < 2.0 (all assets learning)" stop_on_fail="false" module="M-FINE-TUNE" note="If one asset dominates: possible data quality issue with that ticker." />

config/global.yaml §backtest.quality_gates
<verification id="V-M-BACKTEST-01" description="Sharpe ratio: annualized risk-adjusted return on test set" command="python -m src.evaluation.backtest --predictions data/v3/predictions --split test; cat data/v3/results/report.json | jq .sharpe" expected="sharpe >= 0.5 on test split" stop_on_fail="true" module="M-BACKTEST" note="If < 0.0: go to Phase 3 iteration. If 0.0-0.5: weak but positive." />
<verification id="V-M-BACKTEST-03" description="Max Drawdown: maximum peak-to-trough decline" command="cat data/v3/results/report.json | jq .max_dd" expected="max_dd <= 0.25 (<= 25% drawdown)" stop_on_fail="true" module="M-BACKTEST" />
<verification id="V-M-BACKTEST-04" description="PSR (Probabilistic Sharpe Ratio): statistical significance of Sharpe" command="cat data/v3/results/report.json | jq .psr" expected="psr >= 0.7 (high probability Sharpe > 0 is real)" stop_on_fail="false" module="M-BACKTEST" note="If < 0.3: Sharpe not robust — increase MC sample_count." />
<verification id="V-M-BACKTEST-06" description="Walk-forward consistency: val split metrics within 20% of test split" command="python -m src.evaluation.backtest --split val; python -m src.evaluation.backtest --split test; python -c 'compare_val_test()'" expected="|val_sharpe - test_sharpe| / |test_sharpe| < 0.20 (no overfit)" stop_on_fail="false" module="M-BACKTEST" />

docs/reports/audit.md
<verification id="V-LEAK-01" description="Temporal split: train indices < val indices < test indices" command="python -c \"assert train_end_date < val_start_date; assert val_end_date < test_start_date\"" expected="2023-01-01..2025-02-01 < 2025-02-01..2025-09-01 < 2025-09-01..2026-05-01" stop_on_fail="true" module="M-PREPROCESS" reference="audit.md test #5" />
<verification id="V-LEAK-02" description="Feature leakage: no future data in lookback window" command="python -c \"x_df=candles[t:t+506]; y_df=candles[t+506:t+512]; assert x_df.index.max() < y_df.index.min()\"" expected="All lookback bars strictly before first prediction bar" stop_on_fail="true" module="M-PREDICT" reference="audit.md test #6" />
= 600 for d in df.index); assert all(d.hour*60+d.minute <= 1120 for d in df.index)\"" expected="No bars outside 10:00-18:40 MSK (no overnight, pre-market, post-market)" stop_on_fail="true" module="M-PREPROCESS" reference="architecture.md §2.3" />
i\"" expected="Trade exit loop checks actuals[i].high/low, never actuals[i+1]" stop_on_fail="true" module="M-STRATEGY-CORE" reference="audit.md test #8" />

<verification id="V-GRACE-04" description="No stale M-XXX module references (all IDs in development-plan match AGENTS.md)" command="diff <(grep -oP 'M-[A-Z0-9-]+' AGENTS.md | sort -u) <(grep -oP 'M-[A-Z0-9-]+' docs/grace/development-plan.xml | sort -u)" expected="No orphan M-XXX IDs that exist in one file but not the other" stop_on_fail="false" module="M-DOCS" />

verification.expected: Prints usage, all flags, exit codes 0
Prints plan: N tickers × M months, no data fetched
Prints manifest status or 'no manifest found', exit code 0

verification.description: Manifest validation chain: each module validates predecessor manifest
verification.expected: Each module has manifest validation before execution, exits with code 1 on failure

verification_suite.name: Import Integrity
verification_suite.description: Verify all src.* imports resolve without circular dependencies.

verification.expected: Linear dependency: data → kronos → signals → strategies → evaluation. No cycles.

verification_suite.name: Data Quality Assertions

verification.description: No NaN in any OHLCV column
verification.expected: Zero NaN values in any ticker file

verification_suite.verification:
= 500, f'{f} has only {len(df)} bars'\"" expected="All 21 assets have >= 500 bars" stop_on_fail="true" module="M-FETCH" />
<verification id="V-DATA-04" description="Volume > 0 for all bars (zero-volume = holiday or API error)" command="python -c \"import pandas as pd; import glob; for f in glob.glob('data/v3/raw/*.parquet'): df=pd.read_parquet(f); zero_vol=len(df[df['volume']<0]); assert zero_vol==0, f'{f} has {zero_vol} negative volume bars'\"" expected="No negative volume bars. Zero volume ok (holidays)." stop_on_fail="true" module="M-FETCH" />
<verification id="V-DATA-05" description="Amount column computed: close * volume * lot_size (per ticker)" command="python -c \"from config import load_global_yaml; cfg=load_global_yaml(); lots=cfg['lot_sizes']; for ticker,lot in lots.items(): df=pd.read_parquet(f'data/v3/raw/{ticker}.parquet'); expected=df['close']*df['volume']*lot; assert (abs(df['amount']-expected).mean() < 1.0) if 'amount' in df.columns else True\"" expected="Amount column = close * volume * lot_size within tolerance" stop_on_fail="false" module="M-FETCH" />
